title: Give startup a built-in wide placeholder instead of a square eager bind
summary: 0.4.32 publishes `Fa.Player.13.var` with the existing transport and diagnostics scene hardening intact, and adds a generated built-in placeholder texture so startup binds use a deterministic wide front-facing image while video media is still preparing.
reasoning: Live testing showed an additional startup inconsistency: some loads came in with the screen at the expected wide aspect, while others appeared square or visually rear-only before media had fully bound. The current code already admits that the eager `16x16` render texture can produce a transient square bind. This slice avoids adding any external placeholder file by generating a small in-plugin gradient texture and using it only until a real still image or prepared video texture is ready.

changes.summary: Adds a shared generated placeholder texture with a wide aspect ratio for standalone video startup.
changes.reasoning: A deterministic in-plugin placeholder keeps the player free of new external file dependencies and avoids square first-bind presentation.

changes.summary: Uses the built-in placeholder texture as the temporary screen source until real media dimensions and textures are available.
changes.reasoning: The player can now bind a stable front-facing wide image immediately, then refresh cleanly to the real video surface when preparation completes.

changes.summary: Publishes the presentation-hardening candidate under a fresh semantic version and `Fa.Player.13.var` package identity.
changes.reasoning: A new rung keeps startup-presentation evidence separate from the earlier transport and diagnostics-scene slices.

verification: Stamp version 0.4.32 into player.version.json and BuildRuntimeInfo.cs, then build `Fa.Player.13.var` with the diagnostics scene variant still included.
Verify the package contains the 0.4.32 assetbundle, plugin, preset, the normal demo scene, and the packaged diagnostics scene.
Load the player repeatedly and confirm the screen starts with a deterministic wide front-facing placeholder instead of a square transient bind before real media appears.
knownIssues: This slice hardens startup presentation but does not yet claim to solve the Fifth Element media-specific freeze.
The diagnostics scene remains the preferred witness surface for the next Fifth Element log capture.
